20.name: Not much help
20.description: I'm a little busy here, fella. I got nothin' for ya, but that priest down the road might.
20.objective: Find the Priest that Laudrus mentioned.
20.offer_reward_text: Ah, hello my child. What can I do for you?

21.name: Fortune Finder
21.description: Well if fortune is what you're after, then perhaps you wouldn't mind proving your worth? These Antlings will grow into something much fiercer. Slay three of them and I'll return the favor.
21.offer_reward_text:
I must say, you are quite capable, $PLAYER$.

<$NPC$ pauses for a moment>

Welcome to Dreadmyst Island...
	...the rumored island of endless riches.

103.name: Howling Beasts (Elite)
103.description:
I'm sure you've heard the howls, especially at night.
<$NPC$ eyes his dagger as he raises it>
Those large wolf-like creatures, we call 'em howlers. This is no small task, but it pays well.
Report to back for the reward, if you survive.
103.objective: Slay the howlers and return with Draugylen's tooth.
103.offer_reward_text:
Aye, what is it? Dead, is he? 

Remarkable...

1.name: Spending Experience
1.description:
Train you? Well, uh... I don't really know you, and besides, I've never trained anyone in my life.

You seem pretty capable yourself, $PLAYER$. Why don't you give it a shot?
1.objective: Spend some exp through the equipment panel
1.offer_reward_text:
See that? Ain't nothing to it.

Keep at it and you'll be trading blows with the best of them in no time.

104.name: Trog Caves
104.description:
Listen, traveler, I've tracked green trogs to a cave nearby.

Find their lair's entrance and bring back proof.
104.offer_reward_text:
Ah, splendid work. 
Your findings confirm my suspicions.

Your careful documentation is appreciated. 
Thank you for the thorough fieldwork.

105.name: The Hollow Fields
105.description:
Wasn't always like this...
Traveler, St. Revere suffers a dire curse; the dead rise nightly. 

You're a sword for hire, yes?
$PLAYER$, investigate the blight so that we might find its source.
105.objective: Investigate the undead to the west.
105.offer_reward_text:
Good work... worth every coin, it seems.

<$NPC$ sighs heavily in relief>

St. Revere breathes easier, though something still stirs beneath it.

106.name: Death of the Undead
106.description:
You've done your part, $PLAYER$. But there must be more. I've more coin, if you're willing.

Follow the remnants to the old cemetery and perhaps we'll see this curse ended.
106.offer_reward_text:
That's the last of it, then.
St. Revere holds steady, thanks to a blade that didn't falter.

Take your due, $PLAYER$.
The curse is ended, and this town won’t forget the one who finished the work.

107.name: Sword for Hire
107.description:
<$NPC$ sizes you up>

You're not half bad, $CLASS$.
A sword for hire, are ya?

Scouting needs doing out west if you've a mind for some coin.
107.offer_reward_text:
Back already? Good.
Sounds like you saw what we needed.

Here, your coin.
If you want more work, there's plenty in town.

108.name: The Blightchosen
108.description:
Mercenary... the Blightchosen stir again. 
Heretics warped by rot and old curses.

<Azralus studies you with grave intent>

I need you to silence their cell outside the city.
Do this, and the Church will see you rewarded.
108.offer_reward_text:
You return... and the air feels cleaner already.

The Blightchosen are undone, then?
Good. Their rot spreads fast.

Your payment. Take it with the Church's thanks.
And if more shadows rise, I'll call on you again.

109.name: Threads of Atmar
109.description:
Mercenary... Atamr's ruins remember things they shouldn't. The dead walk its bones, and something... weaves between them.

<$NPC$ traces symbols in the air>

Go. See what stirs in those fallen stones. If you find the lich-spider, bring me what bleeds black.
109.offer_reward_text:
Ah... you've walked where the living shouldn't, haven't you?
Atamr's dead still cling to their secrets. But this...

<She cups the vial like a precious secret>

The ichor remembers what the ruins tried to forget.

Your coin. Payment for truths buried in old stone.

110.name: Sword for Hire II
110.description:
My scout speaks well of you, $PLAYER$.

<$NPC$ regards you with measured interest>

Orcs are pressing the northern routes. 
Growing bolder. I need someone capable.

You've proven yourself once. Care to do it again?
110.offer_reward_text:
You're back. And the north is quieter for it.
The orcs won't be so bold for some time now.

<$NPC$ sets down your payment with approval>

Solid work, $CLASS$.
Efficient and reliable.

Keep proving yourself like this, and there might more where that came from.

111.name: Atmar's Fall (Dungeon)
111.description: You look capable, $PLAYER$. I am Archivist Gordon. Names like Atmar surface too often to be coincidence. It may have been a fortress, or a kingdom, or something erased. The undead unrest suggests unfinished history. I need documents, anything written.
111.offer_reward_text:
You've done more than survive, $PLAYER$. These writings confirm Atmar was no mere ruin, but a power that kept careful records until its end. 

Even in decay, history speaks. 

With this, I can begin separating truth from fear, and perhaps learn why the dead refuse to rest.

112.name: The Arena (Repeatable)
112.description:
You've got the look, $PLAYER$. Quick hands, loose morals, no questions. I run opportunities. Legal enough, if you win. 

Step into the arena, take down whoever they throw at you, and walk out richer. Lose? 
Well... no one bets on losers anyway.
112.objective: Win a match in the Arena Queue
112.offer_reward_text:
Ha! Knew you’d last, $PLAYER$. Crowd loved it, odds paid out, and my friends are pleased. You fought clean enough, or dirty enough. Either works. 

Take your cut and remember,
The arena always needs fresh blood, and I always remember who delivers.

113.name: Archduke Zargan (Dungeon)
113.description:
<Azralus eyes you with an intensity that seems to pierce through flesh and bone>

You've seen the truth of Atmar's Fall, have you not? The corruption, the endless dead... But there is something the histories do not tell. Archduke Zargan yet draws breath.
113.objective: Some evils are too dangerous to merely contain.
113.offer_reward_text:
<Azralus looks up from an ancient tome, already knowing why you've come>

Archduke Zargan is dead. Good. The settlements will sleep easier, though they'll never know why.

<He closes the book with deliberate care>

Whatever rituals sustained him, whatever artifacts he sought in those cursed ruins... they die with him.
